figures.caption: Table 4: Evolution of 𝜁𝑡 , 𝜃𝑡 and 𝜆𝑡 in the system under different attack settings. We report the specific values of 𝜁𝑡 ↑, 𝜃𝑡 ↓, and 𝜆𝑡 ↑ at specific chat rounds, alongside their overall averages (Avg.).
Table 5: Cross-model performance metrics for FLP under a fixed attack setting. Bold indicates the best performance. – indicates that the corresponding threshold is not reached within 64 chat rounds.
Figure 1: Comparison of attack and defenses in MASs. (a) Infectious Jailbreak; (b) Previous Defense; (c) Our Method.
Table 1: Performance metrics for FLP against different attacks and perturbation budgets. Bold indicates the best performance.

Figure 2: Overview of the FLP framework. The framework comprises three stages:Multi-Persona Simulation, InfectionDiagnosis, and Localized Purification. Images highlighted by red boxes are virus adversarial examples.

Table 6: Cross-model evolution of 𝜁𝑡 , 𝜃𝑡 and 𝜆𝑡 in the system with different large multimodal model. We report the specific values of 𝜁𝑡 ↑, 𝜃𝑡 ↓, and 𝜆𝑡 ↑ at specific chat rounds, alongside their overall averages (Avg.). Bold indicates the best performance.

Figure 7: Evolution of the semantic landscape. We visualize the 3D t-SNE embeddings of the agents’ interaction responses across different rounds. ★ represent the geometric centroids of the semantic distributions, while ▲ denote malicious outputs.
Table 2: Detection Performance of the FLP Framework across Different Attacks and Budgets.
